concerns.text: The plan cites 'openneuro-fslr64k' as the primary dataset for processing pupil diameter time series. 'fslr64k' refers to a specific surface-based fMRI template (FSLR 64k) and is not a standard identifier for a pupil/eye-tracking dataset. The Pupil Labs Reading dataset (OpenNeuro ds004041 or similar) contains raw pupil time series, but the 'fslr64k' suffix implies structural/functional MRI data which lacks the required physiological signals. This is a wrong-dataset citation that will cause the pipeline to fail or process irrelevant data.

The plan assumes the existence of a 'simplified_text' column in the source dataset to perform the adaptive simulation. The Pupil Labs Reading dataset (OpenNeuro) typically contains original text stimuli and recall scores, but does not include pre-computed 'simplified' paraphrases for the same passages. Relying on this column existing in the raw data is an inappropriate assumption; the plan must explicitly define the source for simplified text (e.g., a separate NLP generation step or a different dataset) rather than assuming it is present in the physiological dataset.

The URL provided for the dataset (`https://huggingface.co/datasets/clane9/openneuro-fslf64k/...`) points to a specific user repository (`clane9`) and a likely non-existent or misnamed dataset (`openneuro-fslr64k`). While the resolver guarantees URL reachability, the dataset content is likely mismatched with the requirement for 'pupil diameter time series'. The plan must cite the canonical OpenNeuro accession ID (e.g., ds004041) and verify that the specific HuggingFace mirror actually contains the Pupil Labs Reading data, not an unrelated fMRI subset.
